footer.description: A hands-on robotics learning experience for kids, teens, adults, parents, and schools.
footer.opensource: Built as a local-first learning project.

hero.heading: Build the robot. Learn the future.
hero.copy: Assemble a personal robot, choose sensors and motion parts, then meet Aiva, the local AI learning coach running on the robot.

brain.title: The Brain Brick
brain.copy: The core module is the robot's computing center inside a technic-brick compatible case. Think of it as the skull: the place where sensing, code, and learning connect.

power.title: Power Up
power.copy: Pick the energy system for your build. The website builder is playful, but the choices represent the modular direction of the real robot.

perception.title: Perception
perception.copy: Select up to three sensors. Cameras, distance sensing, infrared, and LiDAR open different projects and experiments.

motion.title: Motion
motion.copy: Choose how your robot moves. Wheels are simple and fast, tracks are steady, and omni-wheels unlock side-to-side movement.

ai.title: Meet Aiva
ai.copy: Aiva is the robot's personality and learning coach. The LLM, voice experience, and Learning Hub run directly on the robot.

result.title: Build the robot. Learn the future.
result.copy: Save this configuration locally in this browser or start fresh. Nothing in this playful builder is uploaded.

brain_brick.name: Brain Brick
brain_brick.desc: The always-included computing core for code, sensors, and local AI experiments.

battery_standard.name: Standard Battery
battery_standard.desc: A simple power option for focused indoor workshop builds.

battery_extended.name: Extended Battery
battery_extended.desc: Longer runtime for bigger experiments and extended learning sessions.

battery_outdoor.name: Outdoor Charging
battery_outdoor.desc: A rugged power concept for outside projects and longer field tests.

rgb_camera.name: RGB Camera
rgb_camera.desc: Adds basic visual sensing for image projects and robot perspective.

stereo_camera.name: Stereo Camera
stereo_camera.desc: Adds depth perception so the robot can reason about distance.

ultrasound.name: Ultrasound Sensor
ultrasound.desc: Classic distance sensing for obstacle projects and beginner robotics.

infrared.name: Infrared Sensor
infrared.desc: Useful for line tracking, proximity experiments, and simple signals.

lidar.name: LiDAR
lidar.desc: A scanning sensor concept for mapping and autonomous robot projects.

drive_2wd.name: 2-Wheel Drive
drive_2wd.desc: Fast, simple, and agile for early programming challenges.

drive_4wd.name: 4-Wheel Drive
drive_4wd.desc: More stable movement for heavier builds and mixed terrain.

tank_tracks.name: Tank Tracks
tank_tracks.desc: A steady motion platform for terrain experiments.

omni_wheels.name: Omni-Wheels
omni_wheels.desc: A movement concept for 360 degree driving and advanced control.

aiva_coach.name: Aiva Coach
aiva_coach.desc: A local learning coach that explains lessons and helps debug code.

local_voice.name: Local Voice
local_voice.desc: Voice interaction designed to run directly on the robot.

events.title: Hands-on robot building for curious learners.
events.copy: R0607 workshops are coming soon in Berlin. Register interest and we will use age groups to plan suitable cohorts before dates are announced.

learn.a: They will assemble and program a robot, work with sensors and motion, and learn how local AI can support robotics projects.

bring.q: What do learners need to bring?
bring.a: Details will be shared before the first cohort. The goal is to keep the setup practical and guided.

cost.a: Pricing is still TBD while the workshop format is being validated.

list.a: It is an interest list. We will contact you when Berlin cohorts are ready.

form.consent: I agree to be contacted about R0607 workshops.

messages.invalid: Please check the form and try again.
messages.queued: Thanks. Your interest was recorded locally for this build; connect Supabase to store submissions.
messages.registered: Thanks. You are on the interest list.
messages.rate_limited: Too many submissions. Please try again later.
messages.unknown: Something went wrong. Please try again later.

education.title: Robotics and AI learning, built for the classroom.
education.copy: R0607 starts with Berlin workshop cohorts and grows toward future school pilots once the learning material and hardware are stable enough.

programming.title: Programming fundamentals
programming.copy: Learners write code that changes a physical robot they can see, test, and improve.

robotics.title: Robotics and sensors
robotics.copy: Hardware choices connect motors, sensors, perception, and feedback loops.

debugging.title: Debugging and systems thinking
debugging.copy: Students learn to reason about code, mechanics, power, and data together.

ai.title: AI literacy
ai.copy: Local AI makes modern assistant behavior tangible without hiding the system.

teamwork.title: Teamwork
teamwork.copy: Workshops support project-based learning, communication, and iteration.

privacy.title: Privacy-aware computing
privacy.copy: The local-first architecture gives schools a concrete way to discuss responsible technology.

pilot.title: Pilot format
pilot.copy: Small Berlin workshops come first. School pilots can follow after the format is mature, with clear age bands, instructor support, and practical room and equipment expectations.

messages.invalid: Please add a valid name and email.
messages.rate_limited: Too many submissions. Please try again later.
messages.unknown: Something went wrong. Please try again later.

learn.title: Your personal robot learning coach.
learn.copy: R0607 combines a physical robot, local AI, voice, and courses in one on-robot Learning Hub.

personal.title: Your personal robot
personal.copy: The robot is framed as the learner's own build. Choices, projects, and Aiva interaction should feel personal rather than generic.

aiva.title: Aiva
aiva.copy: Aiva is the robot's personality and learning coach. It helps explain lessons, debug code, and suggest next steps in context.

privacy.title: Local-first privacy
privacy.copy: The LLM and voice experience run directly on the robot. AI chat and voice learning interactions are not sent to cloud AI providers.

hub.title: Learning Hub
hub.copy: The Learning Hub is a web app served from the robot. It contains AI chat, voice interaction, courses, guides, and project material.

path.title: Learning path
path.copy: The curriculum moves from basic programming into robotics, game development, machine learning, AI development, and autonomous robots.

parents.title: For parents and schools
parents.copy: The program will explain age guidance, workshop format, safety boundaries, privacy, and what learners need before starting.

intro.title: Overview
intro.copy: R0607 collects as little website data as possible for the launch phase.

signup.title: Workshop interest
signup.copy: The workshop form collects email, age group, language, consent, and timestamp so we can contact interested people about Berlin workshops.

localAi.title: Local AI
localAi.copy: Aiva, the LLM, voice features, and Learning Hub run directly on the robot. AI chat and voice learning data is not sent to cloud AI providers.

rights.title: Deletion
rights.copy: You can request deletion of workshop interest data by contacting hello@r0607.com or by using the unsubscribe link sent with your confirmation email.

hosting.title: Hosting
hosting.copy: The website is intended for Vercel hosting and Supabase in the EU region for workshop interest storage.

operator.heading: Operator
operator.name: [Operator name — to be filled before launch]

note.heading: Note
note.copy: This website is a learning project. Workshop content and product details are subject to change.
